exemplar.title: How effective are Starbucksâ sustainability initiatives in improving the environmental footprint and corporate social responsibility for the year 2023?

detailedExemplarData.title: How effective are Starbucksâ sustainability initiatives in improving the environmental footprint and corporate social responsibility for the year 2023?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Sustainability is correctly chosen and returned to in every major section (SWOT, benchmarking, TBL).
Clear focus on Starbucks with concrete company facts and goals quoted.
Demonstrates a strong conceptual grasp through analytical commentary in key sections.
weaknesses: Lacks an explicit theoretical framing or definition of sustainability in the introduction.
Conceptual integration is uneven; narrative passages sometimes lack depth.
Analysis not woven through every paragraph, limiting full integration.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place this work firmly in band 4: the student shows a strong understanding of sustainability and applies it directly to Starbucks, but missing theoretical framing and partial rather than full integration prevent a band 5 score. A score of 4/5 reflects the clear, consistent use of the concept while acknowledging remaining gaps in depth and framing.

strengths: References five supporting documents, meeting the 3â5 document requirement.
Most sources provide useful company figures and competitor targets.
Documents are directly relevant to the research question and Starbucksâ sustainability context.
weaknesses: Depth is uneven: several statistics are cited without further explanation.
Reliance on a secondary methodology website adds limited insight.
Lacks the range and probing depth to reach the top band.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the student provides the requisite number of documents (band 4 for quantity), the uneven depth and limited analytical insight of some sources align more closely with band 3âs description of ârelevant and sufficiently in-depthâ rather than the top bandâs requirement for breadth and probing analysis. Hence 3/4 is appropriate.

strengths: Selection of SWOT, benchmarking and Triple Bottom Line is appropriate and recognized.
Each tool is applied to Starbucks with clear relevance to the research question.
Adequate demonstration of how tools inform the analysis.
weaknesses: Application is often descriptive rather than interpretive or critical.
No explicit justification for choosing these specific tools over alternatives.
Benchmarking peer selection (NestlÃ©, Unilever) is not theoretically justified.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand feedback rates both selection and application at band 3: the student uses an adequate toolkit applied to Starbucks with general relevance, but the analysis remains largely descriptive and selection rationale is underdeveloped. A score of 3/4 reflects this middle-band performance.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Comparisons with competitors and identification of weaknesses show analytical thinking.
Some evaluation of targets and suggestions for more ambitious goals.
Evidence of moving beyond description by linking SWOT and benchmarking insights.
weaknesses: Data usage is superficialâgoals rather than actual 2023 performance figures.
Many claims lack proper citations, weakening validity.
Analysis is sporadic and would benefit from quantitative evidence integration.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Data selection/use is limited (band 2) while analysis quality meets band 3, resulting in an overall band 3 performance: the student delivers âsome analysisâ but not the sustained, thorough integration required for higher marks. A score of 3/5 balances these strands.

strengths: Explicitly answers the research question, stating Starbucksâ progress and need for greater ambition.
Summarizes key findings and recommendations clearly.
Links back to main analytical sections in broad terms.
weaknesses: Occasionally overstates Starbucksâ success without substantiating evidence.
Conclusions are not consistently tied to specific data points.
Could restate the research question and evidence links more explicitly.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the conclusion answers the research question fully (band 3 for relevance), it only partially aligns with evidence due to overstatement and lack of specific data linkage (band 2 for consistency). Overall band 2 is appropriate, reflecting some consistency but room to strengthen evidence ties.

strengths: Report is well organized with a clear introduction, analysis sections, and conclusion.
Headers and contents page reflect the structure effectively.
Logical flow supports reader orientation throughout.
weaknesses: Contents page lacks page numbers, reducing navigability.
Personal narrative section is marginally relevant to the main analysis.
Minor adjustments to section referencing could improve clarity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The structure is appropriate and coherent throughout (band 2), with only minor navigational enhancements needed. A full mark of 2/2 reflects this consistent organizational quality.

strengths: All required elements (title, word count, research question, bibliography, citations, page numbers) are present.
Adheres strongly to presentation requirements.
Tables and figures are generally clear.
weaknesses: Citation formatting is inconsistent (footnote style varies).
Some bibliography entries lack complete details (e.g., publication dates).
Table formatting could be improved for readability.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All presentation requirements are met fully (band 2), evidencing careful attention to formatting and completeness. Minor stylistic inconsistencies do not detract from overall presentation quality, justifying 2/2.

strandSelections.reasoning: Sections follow a logical order (Introduction-Analysis-Conclusion) and headers match the contents page, indicating an âappropriate structure throughoutâ.

Most sources are directly relevant and provide useful company figures or competitor targets, but depth is uneven: several statistics are cited without explanation, and secondary websites (Research-Methodology) add limited insight.  Overall the documents are ârelevant and sufficiently in-depthâ rather than wide-ranging and probing.
All required elements (title, word count, RQ, bibliography, citation markers, page numbers) are present, so the highest presentation band is earned.

Five supporting documents are referenced (Starbucks impact reports, NestlÃ©, Unilever, CSR article, research-methodology site), placing the student in the 3-5 document range and meeting the highest band for this strand.

Each tool is applied to Starbucks and linked to the research question, yet the application often remains descriptive (e.g., listing targets rather than analysing progress).  Relevance is generally clear but the effectiveness of application is mixed, matching the middle band description.

Data are present (carbon-cut targets, % of C.A.F.E. sourcing, competitor benchmarks) but most are goals rather than 2023 results, and several numerical claims lack sourcing.  This constitutes âsuperficial selection and useâ rather than adequate, so Band 2 is appropriate.

The analysis moves beyond description by comparing Starbucks to rivals, identifying weaknesses, and suggesting more ambitious targets.  Nonetheless, arguments are not consistently developed with evidence and integration is sporadic, fitting âsome analysis of the research questionâ (Band 3).
The student explicitly answers the research question, stating Starbucks has made âsignificant progressâ yet needs greater ambition, thus meeting the top band for this strand.

The student selects three recognised tools (SWOT, Benchmarking, Triple Bottom Line).  This is an adequate but not especially wide or sophisticated toolkit; therefore the work fits the âadequate selectionâ band.

The conclusion broadly aligns with the evidence presented but occasionally overstates Starbucksâ success without substantiating 2023 performance, so only âsome conclusions consistent with evidenceâ is justified.

Starbucks is clearly the focus throughout; every tool (SWOT, benchmarking, TBL) is applied directly to the firm and concrete company facts/goals are quoted.  Nevertheless, data gaps (few 2023 performance figures) prevent a truly comprehensive, fully-integrated treatment, so the strand sits in the âstrong analysis with partial integrationâ band rather than the top band.

The student chooses an approved key concept (sustainability) and returns to it in every major section (SWOT strengths/opportunities, Greener Stores discussion, benchmarking metrics, Triple Bottom Line planet-people-profit).  The commentary shows a solid grasp of what sustainability means for Starbucks, yet the analysis is not woven through every paragraph â some narrative passages (e.g., personal motivation, descriptive CSR benefits) add little conceptual depth.  Hence the work demonstrates a strong understanding with partial, but not fully-integrated, analysis.

annotations.comment: The contents page outlines structure clearly, though adding corresponding page numbers would enhance navigation.
The concept âsustainabilityâ is correctly chosen but would benefit from an explicit definition and theoretical framing in the introduction to strengthen integration.
The research question is clear and focused, but specifying which environmental metrics (e.g., carbon emissions, waste diversion) will be assessed could increase precision.
All required components (title, word count, research question) are present, demonstrating strong adherence to presentation requirements.
The logical structure signaled here supports reader orientation, indicating a coherent organization throughout the assessment.
The introduction provides useful company context, but integrating the sustainability concept with academic literature here would deepen conceptual understanding.
This descriptive overview lacks citations for key claims (e.g., âglobal leaderâ). Including supporting data or references would strengthen validity.
The personal narrative is engaging but marginally relevant; refocusing on organizational factors would better serve your analysis.
Selecting SWOT is appropriate, but explaining why this tool suits the research question would clarify its relevance.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The SWOT items capture key issues; however, the analysis remains mostly descriptive. Linking each point back to sustainability metrics would add depth.
This section lists targets (e.g., 50% carbon reduction) but omits current performance data. Including 2023 results would enhance analysis quality.
Citation formatting in footnotes is inconsistent; standardizing style (APA or MLA) will improve presentation.
The weaknesses discussion identifies valid cost issues but could tie these explicitly to sustainability theory such as costâbenefit analysis.
Opportunities are well-identified but could be prioritized based on potential environmental impact to strengthen evaluation.
Threats section mentions regulations but lacks specific examples; citing recent policy changes would substantiate the argument.
Benchmarking is a suitable tool here, but justifying why NestlÃ© and Unilever were chosen over other peers would clarify selection criteria.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The table compares targets but excludes actual 2023 data. Including current metrics would improve the analysis and evidence base.
This discussion makes good comparisons but would benefit from a quantitative assessment (e.g., % differences) to deepen evaluation.
Table 2 is clear but headings merge into data text; adjusting cell formatting will improve readability.
The truncated sentence here interrupts flow; ensure complete sentences to maintain coherence in tool explanation.
The people discussion is good but mentions criticism without evidence; adding a specific case or source would strengthen the evaluation.
This section uses strong targets but should connect them quantitatively to Starbucksâ 2023 environmental performance for deeper analysis.
The profit analysis provides rationale but lacks financial data (cost savings or revenue impacts). Integrating figures would enhance evaluation.
The conclusion summarizes findings well; explicitly restating the research question and linking each conclusion point back to evidence would strengthen coherence.
Comparisons to NestlÃ© and Unilever are insightful but lack quantified benchmarks; including specific values would reinforce your arguments.
The bibliography covers required sources, but some entries (e.g., Unilever) lack publication dates. Ensure complete citation details for all references.
